content: Most existing discussions treat Self-Refine as a general iterative refinement framework and LLM self-reflection mechanisms as the underlying cognitive logic that enables refinement, but we can reverse this relationship: we can embed multiple inconsistent self-reflection perspectives (a core component of cutting-edge LLM self-reflection mechanisms) directly into the iterative feedback loop of the Self-Refine framework to break its reliance on single-perspective self-judgment. This creates a multi-perspective Self-Refine that preserves the original framework's key advantage of requiring no extra training or labeled data, while reducing the common flaw of getting stuck in local optima caused by consistent blind spots in the model's single self-evaluation perspective.
